- 
Womb (uterus) cancer
Contents 
Overview 
Symptoms 
Causes 
Tests and next steps 
Treatment 
Help and support 
Credit: Created by Multimedia Team at nhs.uk
Womb cancer is cancer that affects the womb (uterus). It's most common in women who've been through menopause. Anyone with a womb can get womb cancer. The womb is where a baby grows during pregnancy. Most womb cancer usually starts in the lining of the womb (endometrium), this is also known as endometrial cancer. How serious the womb cancer is depends on how big it is, if it has spread and your general health. 
Important: 
Get your symptoms checked
It's important to get any symptoms of womb cancer checked as soon as possible. Finding it earlier makes it easier to treat.
Page last reviewed: 29 October 2024
Next review due: 29 October 2027
